conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else feels like a chore now?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting too old for this. I used to be so independent, and now I’m just tired all the time.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like watching someone else’s life. I see friends posting about their weekends and I think, 'That's not my life anymore.'
When you see your friends' posts and think "that's not my life anymore," what do you think your life is like now?
I don't know. It just feels empty. I look at the screen and I think, 'What am I doing with this life?'
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started a few months ago. I used to have things to look forward to, but now I just wake up and wait for the day to be over.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
I used to plan things, like a trip or a project. Now I don't plan anything because I know it won't make a difference.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. I wake up and I don't have anything to look forward to. It feels like I'm just existing, not living.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just keep doing the same things until it gets too hard to keep going.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has this feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about stuff you normally enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently—does that kind of thinking follow you into the day?
Yeah, it does. I catch myself thinking about it during meetings, and I just feel like I'm zoning out.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does it make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel like I’m not good enough. Like I failed, even though I know I didn't exactly fail, but that’s how it feels.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started after the breakup. I used to have a routine, but now I just feel lost.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Work, mostly. I just feel like I’m there to collect a paycheck, but I don't actually want to be there.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't really know. I just know I need to get through them, but I have no idea how I’m going to do that.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It feels like a mix of both. I know I shouldn't give up, but at the same time, I just don't have the energy to keep pushing forward.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
It's definitely more that I feel stuck. I just feel like I’m holding everyone back, and I don't know how to stop doing that.
